frameworks.name: 限制高風險材料
frameworks.desc: X01 Material Restrictions 限制 asbestos、mercury、lead 等已知 hazardous ingredients；例如部分產品類別不可含超過 1,000 ppm asbestos，mercury 不超過 0.1% (1000 ppm) by weight。

frameworks.name: 既有危害與場址風險管理
frameworks.desc: X02 Interior Hazardous Materials Management、X03 CCA and Lead Management 與 X04 Site Remediation 要處理既有建築 asbestos、lead、PCBs、CCA 與 contaminated sites。

frameworks.name: VOCs 與材料透明
frameworks.desc: X05 Enhanced Material Restrictions、X06 VOC Restrictions、X07 Materials Transparency 與 X08 Materials Optimization 把 product documentation、ingredient transparency、emission thresholds 與第三方評估串在一起。

frameworks.name: 廢棄物、害蟲與清潔政策
frameworks.desc: X09 Waste Management、X10 Pest Management and Pesticide Use 與 X11 Cleaning Products and Protocols 對應 hazardous waste、IPM、pesticides、cleaning products 與 maintenance practices。
covers: X10 Pest Management and Pesticide Use

questions.stem: In Part 1: Select Compliant Interior Furnishings of Feature X05: Enhanced Material Restrictions, how much Halogenated flame retardants (HFR) can be found in the textiles and plastics in newly installed furniture, millwork, and fixtures?
questions.source_explanation:
Textiles (i.e., fabrics including upholstery) and plastics in products can contain 100 
ppm (0.01%) by weight or less of the below compounds and chemical classes unless 
higher amounts are mandated by local codes.

questions.stem: In Part 1: Select Compliant Interior Furnishings of Feature X05: Enhanced Material Restrictions, (max 1 Point), newly installed furnishings, built-in furniture, interior finishes, and finish materials must comply by at least what percent compliance by cost to fulfill the requirement?
Which of the following WELL features promotes the use of products that have been independently verified to be free of many suspected hazards?

options.text: Feature X03: CCA and Lead Management

questions.stem: An urban development has recently completed construction, and new tenants are moving in. The project team is tabulating all products (including furnishings, furniture, and finishes) to report for Feature X05: Enhanced Material Restrictions. What metric should the project team use to report compliance?
Feature X11: Cleaning Products and Protocols requires a project develops and implements a cleaning plan. What must this plan detail?

options.text: The time each cleaning function takes to complete
Green cleaning products from regular cleaning products

questions.stem: According to Part 1: Improve Cleaning Practices of Feature X11: Cleaning Products and Protocols in the WELL Building Standard™ (WELL™), the cleaning plan must outline a training program. Who is the training delivered to?

options.text: Management staff and board of directors

questions.stem: A janitorial company is beginning work at a WELL Certified building, and several requirements related to the WELL Building Standard are written into the contract. Since the project has achieved Feature X11: Cleaning Products and Protocols, which of the following requirements must be met?

options.text: Walk-off mats should be wet cleaned every day.
Mops, rags and dusters used to clean all non-porous surfaces must consist of microfiber with a denier no higher than 1.5.
Cleaning products, soaps, shampoos, disinfection and sanitization products are not manufactured with ingredients classified under the Globally Harmonized System (GHS).

questions.stem: Part 2: Select Compliant Architectural and Interior Products in Feature X05: Enhanced Material Restrictions gives 1 point if, at minimum, 50% by cost of flooring components contain halogenated flame retardants at less than
Feature X07: Material Transparency includes Part 3: Select Products with Third-Party Verified Ingredients. What verification method is required through IWBI?

In order to achieve optimization Feature X07: Materials Transparency, at least 50% by count or 25 distinct, permanently installed products and furniture ingredients are disclosed by the manufacturer, a disclosure organization, or a third party through which of the following?
In order to fulfill the requirement of Feature X10: Pest Management and Pesticide Use, how are pests primarily managed?

options.text: Finalizing a pest control use listing
There is no active management of pests

questions.stem: The intent of Feature X12: Contact Reduction is

options.text: to implement strategies to prevent minor impact injuries.
to implement strategies to streamline movement through common areas.
to implement strategies to reduce human contact with respiratory particles and surfaces that may carry pathogens.
to implement strategies to reduce the risk of airborne disease.

questions.stem: What are some of the design features that can be implemented to meet the requirements of Feature X12: Contact Reduction?

options.text: Design and implement physical barriers in the project to prevent respiratory particles.
Require corporate HR policies around mandatory sick days.
Facial tissues provided in all common areas.

questions.stem: All of these are policies in Part 1: Reduce Respiratory Particle Exposure that can be implemented to meet the requirements of Feature X12: Contact Reduction except

options.text: Marks to Increase Distance Between People
Encourage use of many entrances/exists whenever possible to avoid clustering
Create self service systems to control ingress and egress

questions.stem: Regarding Part 2: Address Surface Hand Touch of Feature X12: Contact Reduction, the requirements for hands-free operation must be met for the following functions, except

options.text: Water bottle fillers, water faucets and dispensers.

questions.stem: Which material selection process does not promote the selection of products that have been audited to minimize impacts on human and environmental health as listed in Feature X08: Materials Optimization?

options.text: Living Building Challenge's Red List v4.0
Cradle to Cradle Basic Level restricted Substances List v4
REACH Restriction, Authorization and SHVC list

questions.stem: Why should a project team ensure a waste stream plan is created and followed for a Battery company in Sarasota, Florida that wants to obtain a WELL Certification?

options.text: To comply with the requirements of Feature: X04 Site Remediation.
To improve the recycling rate by occupant.
To comply with the requirements of Feature X09: Waste Management.
To meet the minimum recycling space required.

questions.stem: During a renovation of a project in Sarasota, FL, is demonstration prior remediation of lead paint in the building. What does Feature X02: Interior Hazardous Materials Management require to verify this prior remediation?
questions.source_explanation:
Part 2: Manage Lead Paint Hazards of Feature X02: Interior Hazardous Materials 
Management.

questions.stem: Which of the following actions taken by the project team does not follow the intent of Feature X09: Waste Management?

options.text: Identify sources of waste and create strategies to minimize waste generation.
Creating a recycling and materials recovery plan.
Making the building owner purchase trash bins 30 lb or larger.
Establishing and Identifying Roles and Responsibilities for a Waste Management Plan.

questions.stem: A project team is working on a building in Orlando that was constructed prior to any applicable laws banning or restricting lead and asbestos. How often are they required to revisit strategies for managing existing hazards?
questions.source_explanation: Feature X02: Interior Hazardous Materials Management.

questions.stem: A team in Sarasota is renovating an old Fuel Station into a WELL Certified farm-to-table restaurant. What standard must be used for the site assessment, prior to construction?

options.text: Soil Quality Guidance on Sustainable Remediation Standard
Phase I and Phase II Environmental
San Francisco Reduce Risk Pesticide List

questions.stem: An office building in Minneapolis is working to comply with Part 2: Restrict Mercury of Feature X01: Materials Restrictions. For all spaces, what restrictions are detailed for newly installed fire alarms, meters, sensors, relays, thermostats, and load break switches?

options.text: Contains no more than 0.1% (1000 ppm) of mercury by weight

questions.stem: What is the intent of the Materials Concept within a building?

options.text: To reduce human exposure to chemicals that may impact health during construction, remodeling, furnishing and operation of buildings.
To minimize the impact of volatile organic compounds (VOCs) emitted by products on indoor air quality.
To mitigate environmental contamination and associated exposure to hazards present in certain wastes.
To achieve high levels of indoor air quality across a building's lifetime.

questions.source_explanation: Read the overview for the Mind Concept.

questions.stem: An office building in Jacksonville does not include exterior areas, bare soil, artificial turf, playgrounds, or surfaces containing loose-fill rubber within the project boundary. What verification method would the project team use to document compliance with Part 1: Manage Exterior CCA Hazards of Feature X03: CCA and Lead Management?

WELL Feature X04: Site Remediation requires site assessment, testing, and remediation for the development of contaminated sites. What verification method is required to satisfy the requirements of this feature?
